tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: The India national cricket team represents India in international cricket and is governed by the BCCI.

tab2.title: Achievements
tab2.content: India has won multiple ICC tournaments including the Cricket World Cup and Champions Trophy.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: India is one of the strongest cricket teams with legendary players like Sachin Tendulkar and Virat Kohli.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: The Australian national cricket team is one of the most successful teams in the history of cricket.

tab2.title: Achievements
tab2.content: Australia has won the Cricket World Cup multiple times and dominated international cricket for decades.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Legendary players include Ricky Ponting, Shane Warne and Glenn McGrath.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: England is one of the oldest cricket teams and played the first ever Test match in cricket history.

tab2.title: Achievements
tab2.content: England won the ICC Cricket World Cup in 2019.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: England is considered the birthplace of modern cricket.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Pakistan is known for producing some of the most talented fast bowlers in cricket history.

tab2.title: Achievements
tab2.content: Pakistan won the ICC Cricket World Cup in 1992.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Legendary players include Imran Khan, Wasim Akram and Waqar Younis.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: South Africa is one of the strongest cricket teams known for powerful players.

tab2.title: Achievements
tab2.content: South Africa has produced many world class players across generations.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Famous players include Jacques Kallis, AB de Villiers and Dale Steyn.
